reasoning: Both models skipped the PKGBUILD file after misclassifying it as binary, making safety assessment impossible since PKGBUILD defines build/install behavior.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD (critical build script) was skipped by both models due to erroneous binary file classification
No .install hooks or other executables were reviewed
false_negatives: Entire PKGBUILD content including build()/package() functions were skipped - could contain malicious code, unsafe permissions, or network calls
learnings: Improve binary detection: PKGBUILD should never be classified as binary. Add text file validation heuristics.
Fail-safe mechanism: Abort audit with error if PKGBUILD can't be read rather than skipping
Prioritization: Always force PKGBUILD review first before proceeding to other files
Tool robustness: Add fallback decoding for suspected text files with encoding issues
